ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, 3 pages. All fields legible.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number 'SEG-25D-326856' does not contain an LC reference. No field labeled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar exists.

locations_found: Invoice page 1 footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'
Invoice page 2 footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'
Invoice page 3 footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'
Invoice page 3 company stamp/signature block: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Gustaf Werners gata 2, SE-421 32 Västra Frölunda, Sweden'

criteria_met: Certificate invoice reference 2005654 matches invoice number -2005654 (after normalization)
Certificate consignor 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' identified in invoice footer and header
Certificate consignee 'Luvian company' identified in invoice delivery and invoice address
pairing_validation.motivation: All three pairing criteria are met: invoice number matches, consignor is identified, and consignee is identified. Valid document pair.

consignor.certificate_value: Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 42132, Göteborg, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN (footer)

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' anywhere in invoice files.
comparison_steps.details: Exact match found in invoice footer on all three pages: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'. Also found in page 3 signature block: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB'. Header shows 'Vitrolife' branding.

comparison_steps.description: Verify company name match after normalization.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB'. Invoice footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB'. Exact match.
Certificate: 'Sweden'. Invoice footer: 'SWEDEN'. Match after case normalization. Additionally, VAT number prefix 'SE' in SE556546629801 confirms Sweden per rule 4.1.2.1.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found directly in invoice footer and signature block.
Country verified via explicit address in footer ('SWEDEN') and VAT prefix 'SE'.

consignor.motivation: Certificate consignor 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' is exactly identified in the invoice footer on all pages as 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'. Country is 'Sweden' in both documents. The VAT number prefix SE further confirms Sweden. MATCH per rule 4.1.0 and 4.1.4.
consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate text: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 42132, Göteborg, Sweden'. Invoice text (footer all pages): 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'. Company name is identical after normalization. Country is 'Sweden' vs 'SWEDEN' – identical after case normalization. Minor difference in postal code format (42132 vs 400 92) does not affect consignor verification per rule 4.1.5 (address differences do not cause MISMATCH). Rule 4.1.0 applies: consignor name found in invoice.

consignee.certificate_value: Luvian company, Vital Village - Villa No.55, Zhulian Sharifi, 44001 Erbil, Iraq
consignee.invoice_value: Luvian Company, vital village house number 55, IQ-Erbil, Iraq (Invoice address)

comparison_steps.description: Search for Invoice to / Bill to / Sold to / Buyer field in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice has 'Invoice address' field containing 'Luvian Company, vital village house number 55, IQ-Erbil, Iraq'.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate consignee name with invoice address name.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: 'Luvian company'. Invoice address: 'Luvian Company'. Identical after case normalization.

rules_applied.description: Invoice address (primary billing field) used as authoritative consignee field.
Company name verified after case normalization.

consignee.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Luvian company' matches 'Luvian Company' in the invoice's 'Invoice address' field after case normalization. Country is 'Iraq' in both documents. Address details differ in format ('Vital Village - Villa No.55' vs 'vital village house number 55') but per rule 4.2.4 address differences do not cause MISMATCH when company name and country match. MATCH.
consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate: 'Luvian company' with country 'Iraq'. Invoice 'Invoice address' field: 'Luvian Company' with 'Iraq'. Name match after case normalization: 'luvian company' = 'luvian company'. Country match: 'iraq' = 'iraq'. Delivery address also confirms: 'Luvian company' in Iraq. Per 4.2.0.2, the Invoice address is the primary billing field and takes priority. MATCH.

goods_description.certificate_value:
4 cartons
Reproductive Media Kits for IVF
See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730
goods_description.invoice_value: Invoice -2005654, various IVF products (G-TL, G-MOPS PLUS, G-IVF PLUS, EmbryoGlue, G-PGD, RapidVit Cleave, etc.), with summary: 'Componets for culturing media & medical instruments for In Vitro Fertilisation IVF'

comparison_steps.description: Certificate contains general goods description with explicit invoice reference. Check if reference matches.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate states 'See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730'. Invoice number is -2005654 (normalized: 2005654) – matches. Invoice shipping date is 20250731 (2025-07-31). Certificate date reference is 20250730 (2025-07-30). Difference is exactly 1 day.

comparison_steps.description: Check if date difference is within 1 day tolerance.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate references date 2025-07-30. Invoice shipping date is 2025-07-31. Difference is exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1, a one-day discrepancy results in MANUAL_REVIEW. However, need to check if invoice has another date field matching 2025-07-30.

comparison_steps.description: Check all date fields in invoice for an exact match to certificate date 20250730.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice header 'Due date: 20250830' – does not match. Invoice 'Shipping date: 20250731' – 1 day difference. However, per 4.3.5.1 the 1-day rule gives MANUAL_REVIEW. But let me check: the certificate says 'dated 20250730'. The invoice number is 2005654. The invoice 'Our order no' is also 2005654. The shipping date on invoice is 20250731. The difference is exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1 this should give MANUAL_REVIEW for this checkpoint.

comparison_steps.description: Check that invoice goods are consistent with certificate description 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF'.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice contains various IVF culture media products (G-TL, G-MOPS PLUS, G-IVF PLUS, EmbryoGlue, RapidVit, RapidWarm, HYASE-10X, HSA-solution, ICSI, SpermRinse, OVOIL, OVOIL HEAVY, G-PGD). Summary at bottom of page 3: 'Componets for culturing media & medical instruments for In Vitro Fertilisation IVF'. These are clearly reproductive media kits/components for IVF. Certificate's 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF' is consistent with the invoice content.

rules_applied.description: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens. Certificate contains explicit reference to invoice 2005654 with 'See attached invoice'. Invoice number matches. Goods are consistent.
Date discrepancy within 1 day. Certificate date 20250730 vs invoice shipping date 20250731. Exactly 1 day difference.

goods_description.motivation: Certificate uses general goods description 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF' with explicit reference 'See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730'. Invoice number 2005654 matches exactly. The date referenced (2025-07-30) differs by exactly 1 day from the invoice shipping date (2025-07-31). Per rule 4.3.5.1, a one-day date discrepancy should result in MANUAL_REVIEW. However, the invoice content (IVF culture media and medical instruments) is fully consistent with the certificate description. Given that rule 4.3.5.1 states the result for a 1-day discrepancy shall be MANUAL_REVIEW, I must apply this rule. But I need to consider: the certificate references '20250730' – this could be a date the invoice was prepared vs the shipping date. The invoice has 'Shipping date: 20250731'. There is no other date in the invoice that matches 20250730 exactly. Per 4.3.5.1, a 1-day difference → MANUAL_REVIEW for this element.
goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate references invoice '2005654 dated 20250730'. Invoice number '-2005654' normalizes to '2005654' – match. The only potentially matching date in the invoice is shipping date 20250731 = 2025-07-31. Certificate date = 2025-07-30. Difference = exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1: 'Om certifikatets fakturadatum och fakturans datum skiljer sig med exakt ett (1) dygn ska resultatet vara MANUAL_REVIEW — inte MISMATCH.' This means this checkpoint should be MANUAL_REVIEW. Invoice goods (IVF culture media products) are consistent with certificate's 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF'. No article numbers in certificate to verify. Re-evaluating: the invoice reference number matches perfectly. The 1-day date difference triggers MANUAL_REVIEW per 4.3.5.1.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. This is the primary verification quantity.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.1 - Direct identification of box 7 value in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Search for 66.40 (or 66,4) as gross weight in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice header states 'Gross weight (kg): 66,4'. Certificate states 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. 66,40 = 66.40 = 66.4 after allowed decimal truncation (removing trailing zero). Both are gross weight in kg. Exact match.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.3.1 - Check for contradictions in other quantities
comparison_steps.description: Invoice also states Net weight (kg): 35,5. Certificate does not state a net weight. No contradiction since certificate only states gross.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate mentions only gross weight. Invoice mentions both gross (66,4) and net (35,5). No NW value in certificate to contradict.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 6 states '4 cartons'. Invoice states 'Number of cartons: 4'.
comparison_steps.details: Both documents state 4 cartons. Additional confirmation.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 quantity 'Gross: 66,40 kg' is the primary verification quantity.
Direct identification: 66.40 kg GW in certificate matches 66.4 kg GW in invoice after trailing zero normalization.
Weight category GW is explicitly stated in certificate ('Gross'). Invoice explicitly states 'Gross weight (kg)'. Categories match.
Numeric value 66.40 identified in invoice as 66.4 – match after allowed decimal truncation.

quantity.motivation: Certificate box 7 states 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. Invoice header states 'Gross weight (kg): 66,4'. After normalizing the trailing zero (66,40 → 66,4), values are identical. Weight category (Gross) matches explicitly in both documents. Unit (kg) matches. Additionally, certificate box 6 states '4 cartons' and invoice states 'Number of cartons: 4' – further confirmation. No contradictions found. MATCH.
quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 'Gross: 66,40 kg' → numeric value 66.40, unit kg, category GW. Invoice: 'Gross weight (kg): 66,4' → numeric value 66.4, unit kg, category GW. 66.40 = 66.4 (trailing zero normalization, per allowed decimal truncation rule 4.4.3). Categories both GW. Units both kg. MATCH. Invoice also has NW 35.5 kg but certificate does not claim any NW, so no contradiction per 4.4.3.1.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin indication in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice page 1 goods table header: 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. This is an explicit dedicated origin field.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'European Community'. Invoice states 'Sweden'. Check if Sweden is an EU member state.
comparison_steps.details: Sweden (SE) is a current EU member state. Per rule 4.5.4.1, when certificate states 'European Community' and invoice states an EU member state, this is verified.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate = 'European Community' (=EU). Invoice = 'Sweden' (EU member). Per 4.5.4.2: cert=EU, faktura=EU-land → verifierad.
comparison_steps.details: Symmetrical rule: EU ↔ member state. Certificate says EU, invoice says Sweden (member state) → verified.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 - Check for non-EU origins in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Check if invoice contains any non-EU origin countries not listed in certificate.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice states only 'Country of Origin: Sweden' as a single unified origin for all goods. Sweden is EU member. No non-EU origins present.

rules_applied.description: EU normalization: 'European Community' in certificate is equivalent to any EU member state in invoice.
EU/member state equivalence: cert='European Community', invoice='Sweden' (EU member) → verified.

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate states origin 'European Community' (box 3). Invoice states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sweden is a current EU member state. Per rules 4.5.4.1 and 4.5.4.2, 'European Community'/'European Union' in certificate is verified when invoice states an EU member state as origin. No non-EU origins present in invoice. MATCH.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'European Community'. Invoice goods table: 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sweden is in EU-27 member list (SE). Per 4.5.4.2: 'Om certifikatet anger EU/European Union/European Community och fakturan anger ett specifikt EU-medlemsland → verifierad.' This is a direct application. Only one origin in invoice (Sweden), which is EU. No non-EU origins to conflict. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: No LC reference found in certificate. Standard verification applies.
Document A is certificate (COO), Document B is invoice. Certificate file has 1 page, invoice file has 3 pages. Single invoice identified with number 2005654.
PASSED. All three criteria met: invoice number matches (2005654), consignor found in invoice (Vitrolife Sweden AB), consignee found in invoice (Luvian Company).

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name search in all invoice files
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH. 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' found in invoice footer and signature block on all pages.
Sweden confirmed via explicit footer address ('SWEDEN') and VAT prefix 'SE'.
MATCH. Sweden = SWEDEN after case normalization.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Consignee priority order – Invoice address as primary
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: Invoice has 'Invoice address' field with 'Luvian Company'. This is the primary billing field.
MATCH. 'Luvian company' = 'Luvian Company' after case normalization.
MATCH. Name and country verified. Address format differences (Vital Village - Villa No.55 vs vital village house number 55) do not cause MISMATCH per rule.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: General goods description with invoice reference
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: Certificate has explicit reference 'See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730'. Invoice number 2005654 matches. Goods are IVF-related, consistent with certificate description.

Certificate references date 2025-07-30. Invoice shipping date is 2025-07-31. Difference is exactly 1 day. Per this rule, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, reassessing: the invoice number matches perfectly and the goods content is clearly consistent. The 1-day difference is between the certificate's referenced date and the invoice's shipping date – these may represent different events (invoice preparation vs shipping). Per the strict rule 4.3.5.1, the result for this specific element is MANUAL_REVIEW.
Box 7 contains 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. This is the verification-grounding quantity.
MATCH. 66.40 kg GW in certificate = 66.4 kg GW in invoice.
Certificate box 6: '4 cartons'. Invoice: 'Number of cartons: 4'. Additional confirmation.
MATCH. 'European Community' verified via 'Sweden' (EU member state).

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The date difference (20250730 vs 20250731) indicates a different invoice
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The invoice number 2005654 matches exactly. Only one invoice exists with this number. The 1-day difference is between the date referenced in the certificate and the invoice's shipping date, which can represent different stages of the shipping/documentation process.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Invoice number prefix dash (-2005654) means a different number
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The dash prefix in '-2005654' is a formatting artifact. The numeric content '2005654' matches the certificate's reference '2005654' exactly. The 'Our order no' field in the invoice also confirms '2005654'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Consignee address mismatch (Vital Village - Villa No.55 vs vital village house number 55)
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.2.4, address differences do not cause MISMATCH when company name and country match. Both refer to the same location in Erbil, Iraq. 'Villa No.55' and 'house number 55' are format variations of the same address.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla uppgifter i certifikatet kan i huvudsak verifieras mot fakturan, men det finns en datumavvikelse på exakt en dag: certifikatet hänvisar till fakturadatum 2025-07-30 medan fakturans leveransdatum är 2025-07-31. Denna mindre avvikelse kräver manuell bedömning för att bekräfta att det rör sig om samma transaktion.
blocking_issues: Goods description: Certificate references date 20250730 but invoice shipping date is 20250731 (1-day discrepancy). Per rule 4.3.5.1 this requires MANUAL_REVIEW.
overall_assessment.summary: Consignor MATCH (Vitrolife Sweden AB verified in invoice footer). Consignee MATCH (Luvian Company verified in invoice address, Iraq). Goods description requires MANUAL_REVIEW due to 1-day date discrepancy between certificate reference date (2025-07-30) and invoice shipping date (2025-07-31). Quantity MATCH (Gross 66.40 kg verified, 4 cartons verified). Country of origin MATCH (European Community verified via Sweden as EU member). Overall: MANUAL_REVIEW due to goods description date discrepancy.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot faktura

sections.certificate_value: Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 42132, Göteborg, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN (footer)
sections.rule_applied: Consignor-namn identifierat direkt i fakturans sidfot på alla sidor. Land verifierat via adress och VAT-prefix SE.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' återfinns exakt i fakturans sidfot på samtliga sidor. Land 'Sweden' bekräftas av explicit adress och VAT-nummer med prefix SE.

sections.certificate_value: Luvian company, Vital Village - Villa No.55, 44001 Erbil, Iraq
sections.invoice_value: Luvian Company, vital village house number 55, IQ-Erbil, Iraq
sections.rule_applied: Mottagarens namn och land verifierade mot fakturans faktureringsadress (Invoice address). Adressskillnader påverkar ej bedömningen.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'Luvian company' matchar 'Luvian Company' i fakturans Invoice address-fält efter normalisering av versaler. Land 'Iraq' överensstämmer exakt.

sections.certificate_value: 4 cartons, Reproductive Media Kits for IVF, See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730
sections.invoice_value: Invoice -2005654, shipping date 20250731, IVF culture media products
sections.rule_applied: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens. Fakturanummer matchar exakt. Datum skiljer sig med exakt 1 dag (20250730 vs 20250731).
sections.motivation: Certifikatet hänvisar till faktura 2005654 daterad 20250730. Fakturanumret matchar exakt (2005654). Dock skiljer sig det refererade datumet (2025-07-30) med exakt en dag från fakturans leveransdatum (2025-07-31). Varuinnehållet (IVF-media) är fullt konsistent med certifikatets beskrivning. Endagsavvikelsen kräver manuell verifiering.

sections.rule_applied: Direkt identifiering av bruttovikt i faktura. Numeriskt värde och viktkategori överensstämmer.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets bruttovikt 66,40 kg matchar fakturans 66,4 kg (efterföljande nolla normaliserad). Viktkategori Gross överensstämmer explicit. Antal kartonger (4) bekräftas av båda dokumenten.

sections.rule_applied: EU-normalisering: European Community i certifikatet verifieras genom att fakturan anger EU-medlemsstaten Sverige som ursprungsland.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'European Community' som ursprung. Fakturan anger 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sverige är EU-medlemsstat. Per EU-normaliseringsregeln är detta verifierat. Inga icke-EU-ursprungsländer förekommer i fakturan.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_notes: Goods description checkpoint is MANUAL_REVIEW due to 1-day date discrepancy (rule 4.3.5.1). This triggers overall MANUAL_REVIEW. All other checkpoints are MATCH. Confidence for goods description (0.82) is below 0.85 threshold for IDENTICAL, which independently confirms MANUAL_REVIEW is correct per section 7 confidence floor rule.
